articles.title: Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’
articles.summary: Jury selection began in Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman over OpenAI disputes. Many prospective jurors expressed negative opinions about Musk, complicating the trial process.

articles.title: Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube
articles.summary: Google is testing an AI chatbot search feature for YouTube called 'Ask YouTube' that enables conversational searches across videos, shorts, and text. The feature is currently available to US YouTube Premium subscribers aged 18+.

articles.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
articles.summary: Canonical plans to integrate AI features into Ubuntu Linux over the next year, offering both enhancements to existing functionality and new AI-native features and workflows.

articles.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
articles.summary: OpenAI secured concessions from Microsoft allowing product sales on AWS while Microsoft gains increased revenue sharing. The agreement resolves legal concerns over OpenAI's $50B Amazon partnership.

articles.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
articles.summary: David Silver's new AI lab Ineffable Intelligence raised $1.1B to develop AI that learns without human data. The startup achieved a $5.1B valuation within months of launch.

articles.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
articles.summary: Skye's AI home screen app for iPhone secured investor backing before launch, indicating growing interest in AI-enhanced iPhone experiences.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted 2 million developers. The funding capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud providers.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring stunt featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment strategy attracted thousands of candidates and helped the startup scale its AI customer interview platform.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A compromised open source package with 1M monthly downloads stole user credentials including warehouse keys, API tokens, and SSH keys after attackers exploited a developer account vulnerability to push malicious code.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware after administrators failed to maintain proper domain records. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption to protect scrambled files against quantum computer attacks. This marks the first confirmed ransomware family using post-quantum cryptography standards.

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face trial over OpenAI's conversion to for-profit status. The court could determine if the company can operate as for-profit and potentially remove current leadership before the anticipated IPO.

articles.title: The missing step between hype and profit
articles.summary: AI industry lacks clarity on the path from hype to actual profitable outcomes, with activists questioning what happens between AI development and real-world implementation.

articles.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
articles.summary: Enterprises struggle to deploy AI at scale due to fragmented data infrastructure. Unified, governed data systems are essential for effective AI implementation beyond consumer-facing tools.

articles.title: Adaptive Ultrasound Imaging with Physics-Informed NV-Raw2Insights-US AI
articles.summary: New AI system NV-Raw2Insights-US uses physics-informed learning to enhance ultrasound imaging quality and adaptability, improving diagnostic capabilities through advanced neural network processing.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: OpenAI's Privacy Filter enables developers to build scalable web applications while protecting user data. The tool filters sensitive information before processing, ensuring privacy compliance at scale.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables AI agents to process and utilize vast amounts of information effectively, advancing practical applications of long-context language models.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring upcoming developments and trends in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling tasks.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology accessible to developers and farmers for sustainable farming innovation.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with open registration. This vibe coding course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Google Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing your home and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads with specialized hardware designed for machine learning tasks. The video demonstrates how these processors efficiently handle complex AI computations.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific discoveries using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by allowing independent local updates across nodes before periodic synchronization, improving fault tolerance and efficiency.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide leverage frontier AI technology for business transformation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family with MoE architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI filter enhancing real-time video game graphics with photorealism. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization tools from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Google warns malicious web pages are poisoning AI agents
articles.summary: Google researchers warn that malicious web pages embed hidden instructions to hijack enterprise AI agents through indirect prompt injections. These invisible commands execute when AI systems scrape infected pages for information.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need interaction infrastructure to manage autonomous AI agents operating across networks. Band, a startup, raised $17M to build a dedicated layer governing how independent AI agents coordinate and share data securely.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret volatile market behavior, finding value in unpredictable patterns that traditional static datasets cannot capture.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from Penn State and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture that improves autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers through supply chain vulnerabilities.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but sacrifices generalization ability. Condensing reasoning diminishes models' capacity to explore alternative solutions for complex problems.

articles.title: How to Build a Lightweight Vision-Language-Action-Inspired Embodied Agent with Latent World Modeling and Model Predictive Control
articles.summary: Tutorial on building an embodied agent using vision-language-action approach with a lightweight world model trained on pixel observations. The agent uses model predictive control in latent space to plan and execute actions in a simulated grid world.

articles.title: Meet Talkie-1930: A 13B Open-Weight LLM Trained on Pre-1931 English Text for Historical Reasoning and Generalization Research
articles.summary: Talkie-1930 is a 13-billion parameter open-weight language model trained exclusively on pre-1931 English text, creating a 'vintage' LLM with historical knowledge cutoff. Researchers developed this model to study historical reasoning and generalization without modern knowledge.

articles.title: Build a Reinforcement Learning Powered Agent that Learns to Retrieve Relevant Long-Term Memories for Accurate LLM Question Answering
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building a reinforcement learning agent that retrieves relevant long-term memories for LLM question answering. Using PPO training and embeddings, the agent learns to select useful memories beyond simple similarity search.

articles.title: My 5 favorite open source operating systems that aren't Linux
articles.summary: The article explores five open-source operating systems that offer alternatives to Linux, ranging from legacy systems to emerging projects.

articles.title: This hidden TV feature tracks your viewing - here's how to turn it off (no matter what brand)
articles.summary: Samsung, LG, and Sony TVs contain hidden tracking features that monitor your viewing habits, posing privacy risks. Users can disable these features across all brands.

articles.title: 77% of IT managers say their AI agents are out of control - 5 ways to rein in yours
articles.summary: Most IT managers report losing control over AI agents, with unsanctioned applications proliferating across organizations. The article provides strategies to manage and govern AI deployments.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization using machine learning techniques integrated with scikit-learn. The approach simplifies building efficient summarization models for NLP applications.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models is becoming accessible beyond major tech companies, democratizing AI development and enabling more developers to create custom agents.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular lightweight framework for serving machine learning models due to its speed and ease of use.

articles.title: A Career in Data Is Not Always a Straight Line, and That’s Okay
articles.summary: Data science careers require flexibility as a crucial skill. The article discusses navigating non-linear career paths and the risks of over-relying on AI agents for decision-making.

articles.title: How Spreadsheets Quietly Cost Supply Chains Millions
articles.summary: Spreadsheets cause supply chain inefficiencies as forecast changes propagate through planning teams, resulting in significant losses between sales and store operations.

articles.title: Comparing Explicit Measures to Calculation Groups in Tabular Models
articles.summary: The article discusses using calculation groups and UDFs as alternatives to explicit measures in tabular models, exploring their benefits for report creators.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments are using it to achieve significant productivity gains.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now displays in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, making it easier for gamers to discover available titles in their cloud gaming library.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: The article explores methods for understanding the early universe's formation and structure. It examines scientific approaches to interpreting cosmic origins and development.

articles.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
articles.summary: Amazon Quick Flows automates repetitive tasks using AI workflows built with natural language, requiring no coding expertise. It helps teams save time on data copying and formatting by intelligently automating routine business processes.

articles.title: Build and deploy an automatic sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
articles.summary: Learn how to build an automated sync solution for Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases that detects S3 events and triggers ingestion jobs, keeping your foundation models updated with real-time data.

articles.title: Build Strands Agents with SageMaker AI models and MLflow
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI enables enterprises to build AI agents with greater control over performance, cost, and compliance compared to managed services. Organizations can deploy models with customized compute resources and integrate them with orchestration frameworks like Bedrock.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models optimized for efficient million-token context inference, with V4-Pro featuring 1.6T total parameters and 49B active parameters.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without extensive code refactoring, allowing organizations to train AI models across distributed data sources while maintaining data privacy and security.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated 600,000+ lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating AI's capability in rapid iteration and testing for machine learning success.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations view social media advertisements across platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged unique perspectives to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through virtual state lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models through feature attribution analysis, advancing AI interpretability and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses noisy measurements and noise models to optimize optical systems for distinguishing objects. The approach involves encoders mapping objects to images which are then corrupted by noise.
